stem: As used in the passage, "model" most nearly means
explanation: In this context, "model" means to base or pattern something after an example. The passage indicates that other schools are using the successful recycling program as a template or foundation for creating their own similar programs. The phrase "model their own recycling efforts after this successful example" clearly shows that the schools are basing their programs on the original school's approach.

distractorRationale.A: This is the correct answer - 'model' here means to base or pattern after something.
distractorRationale.B: While 'model' can mean to display or show off, this doesn't fit the context of schools creating their own programs.
distractorRationale.C: Though 'model' can mean to pose (as in modeling for photographs), this meaning is unrelated to the context of schools developing programs.
distractorRationale.D: While a 'model' can be a miniature representation, this noun form doesn't match the verb usage in the passage.

passage: The school's new recycling program has been remarkably successful in its first month. Students have embraced the initiative with enthusiasm, and the amount of waste diverted from landfills has exceeded all expectations. The program's success can be attributed to clear instructions posted throughout the building and the dedicated efforts of student volunteers who help sort materials during lunch periods. As word of the program's effectiveness spreads, other schools in the district are beginning to <u>model</u> their own recycling efforts after this successful example.
